name: Frozen Peach Torte with Almond Crust
recipeIngredient: 6 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Coarsely chop nuts with brown sugar, flour, and salt in a processor.
3. Add butter, process until the mixture is finely chopped. Press half of the mixture over the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Press the remaining half over the bottom of a 9-inch cake pan.
4. Bake until crusts are golden, about 20 minutes. Transfer to racks and cool completely.
5. Break the crust in pans into crumbs.
6. Place 1 tablespoon water in a small bowl. Sprinkle gelatin over; let stand until gelatin softens, about 10 minutes.
7. Whisk yolks, sugar, kirsch, and 3 tablespoons water in a medium metal bowl until blended. Set bowl over a saucepan of simmering water; whisk constantly until the mixture registers 160°F on a candy thermometer, about 4 minutes. Remove bowl from the hot water.
8. Add gelatin, then white chocolate and whisk until gelatin dissolves and chocolate melts.
9. Let stand until the mixture is cool but not set, whisking occasionally, about 10 minutes.
10. Beat cream in a large bowl until soft peaks form. Fold yolk mixture into cream.
11. Spread one-third of the mousse over the crust in the springform pan. Sprinkle half of the chopped peaches over. Sprinkle half of the crumbs over the peaches.
12. Drop one-third of the mousse by tablespoons over the peaches, spacing evenly. Spread the mousse carefully over, covering the crumbs. Sprinkle with the remaining chopped peaches and crumbs. Carefully spread the remaining mousse evenly over.
13. Cover and freeze overnight. (Can be made 3 days ahead. Keep frozen.)
14. Mix peach slices and powdered sugar in a medium bowl. Let stand for 15 minutes. Using a knife, cut around the pan sides to loosen the torte. Release the pan sides; transfer the torte to a platter.
15. Arrange peaches in concentric circles atop the torte. Let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes and serve.
